interactions.summary: The host asks whether Jiang became curious about Russian and Chinese literature, including Tolstoy and Dostoevsky.
Jiang says Yale gave him a broad education, including Tolstoy, Anna Karenina, Marx, Lenin, and a Russian history course.
The host connects Jiang's ideology/class analysis to Russian and European readings, then asks whether he encountered Yale secret societies such as Skull and Bones.

Jiang says Yale itself had a hierarchy, with Skull and Bones at the top; private-school students knew Yale was partly a social-worth contest, while he naively pursued education and watched the preordained competition from the margins.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang with host correction on membership count

speaker_notes.note: The host corrects Jiang's Skull and Bones membership count; Jiang accepts uncertainty between 12 and 15.

claims.claim: Jiang says Yale's formal education exposed him to Russian literature, Marx, Lenin, and Russian history as part of a broad liberal education.
Jiang describes elite universities as social hierarchies where secret societies certify social worth, and says he was too naive and marginalized to play that game.
claims.temporal_scope: Retrospective model of Yale social power.

signature_moments.moment: Yale's hidden curriculum is not books but proving social worth to a hierarchy already known by boarding-school entrants.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the very point of a university is to prove your social worth
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It marks the first break between Jiang's educational idealism and his later power analysis.

signature_moments.moment: The excluded editor starts his own secret society, making the ritual competition look absurd rather than majestic.
signature_moments.source_phrase: he was so angry that he was not tapped... that he started his own secret society
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It exposes elite ritual as status anxiety in institutional form.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's description of what elite university politics are actually proving beneath the official educational mission.
Selection into a Yale secret society by being chosen rather than applying.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang places himself in Yale class of 1999 and connects that class's reputation to his own admission story.

uncertainty_notes.note: Jiang is uncertain whether Skull and Bones selected 12 or 15 members in the relevant year; preserve that uncertainty.
